title: Reading and Use of English – Part 8
description: You are going to read an article about four people who attended a course on traditional instrument making. For questions 47–56, choose from the people A–D. The people may be chosen more than once.

content.title: The Soul of the Wood: Aspiring Luthiers

texts.A:
### Marco, The Violin Maker
'I’ve played the violin for years, but building one was a different beast,' Marco says. [51]The most daunting task was the precise carving of the scroll, where the slightest slip of the chisel could ruin months of work.[/51] [48]I was surprised to find that my general focus and patience during my day job as a surgeon improved immensely.[/48] [53]I learned that every piece of wood has its own voice, and you have to find it through trial and error.[/53] [50]I realized that my background in anatomy helped me understand the organic curves and structural integrity of the instrument's body.[/50] [47]Initially, the intense smell of the various resins and varnishes was quite overwhelming for my senses.[/47] It was a profound journey into the heart of music.
texts.B:
### Sarah, The Acoustic Guitar Craft
Sarah built her own guitar from a kit under professional guidance. [49]The experience taught me that the quality of an instrument is as much about the intent of the maker as the materials used.[/49] [52]I hadn't anticipated the overwhelming surge of emotion I’d feel when I first plucked a string and heard it resonate.[/52] [47]I was quite startled by how much the physical vibration of the tools during sanding affected my hands in the first week.[/47] [54]What I valued most was the mentorship of the master luthier, who could diagnose a flaw just by tapping the wood.[/54] [56]If I hadn't seen a documentary on craftsmanship, I never would have thought this was possible for an amateur.[/56] It was a creative epiphany.
texts.C:
### Victor, The Traditional Flute Maker
Victor learned to make wooden flutes. 'It’s about the breath,' he notes. [51]I struggled with the mathematical complexity of calculating the exact placement of the finger holes for perfect intonation.[/51] [48]I noticed that my respiratory health and lung capacity improved, which was a great side effect of testing the instruments.[/48] [53]I realized that making an instrument is a dialogue between the laws of physics and the artistry of the maker.[/53] [50]I found that the meticulousness I used in my previous career as an engineer was perfect for the precision drilling required.[/50] [55]It took a while to adjust to the isolation of the workshop, which I found quite lonely at first.[/55] [52]I’d initially worried I was too unmusical to succeed.
texts.D:
### Elena, The Percussion Workshop
Elena specialized in making traditional hand drums. [49]The project made me realize that we are surrounded by rhythm if we only take the time to listen to the materials.[/49] [56]I wouldn't have found the workshop if my local community center hadn't advertised it as a heritage preservation project.[/56] [54]I was moved by the camaraderie of the other participants, who helped each other stretch the skins even late into the night.[/54] [47]I hadn't expected the physical exhaustion of the repetitive hammering to be so draining initially.[/47] [55]I found the initial task of preparing the raw animal hides incredibly messy and somewhat repulsive.[/55] [52]I’d been concerned that I wouldn't be strong enough for the heavy construction.

questions.question: mentions being surprised by the physical or sensory impact of the workshop environment or tools?
questions.explanation: Marco (A) mentions the resins/varnishes, Sarah (B) the tool vibration, and Elena (D) the exhaustion of hammering.

questions.question: notes a positive change in their professional life or physical health as a result of the craft?
questions.explanation: Marco (A) saw his surgical focus improve, and Victor (C) improved his respiratory health.

questions.question: expresses a change in their perspective on the importance of maker intent or natural rhythm?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) reflects on the maker's intent, and Elena (D) on being surrounded by rhythm.

questions.question: mentions how their professional background or skills were advantageous to the instrument's construction?
questions.explanation: Marco (A) used his anatomy background, and Victor (C) his engineering meticulousness.

questions.question: describes a challenge related to technical precision or mathematical calculations?
questions.explanation: Marco (A) mentions carving the scroll, and Victor (C) the calculation of hole placement.

questions.question: admits to having had prior doubts or having an intense emotional reaction to the first sound?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) felt intense emotion hearing the first string, and Victor (C) worried he was too unmusical.

questions.question: reflects on a broader philosophical lesson learned about the material's voice or the laws of physics?
questions.explanation: Marco (A) learned about the wood's voice, and Victor (C) about physics vs. artistry.

questions.question: highlights the value of the guidance of a master or the collaborative spirit of the group?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) valued her mentor's expertise, and Elena (D) the camaraderie of participants.

questions.question: describes a period of frustration or adjustment to the isolation or messy preparation?
questions.explanation: Victor (C) adjusted to the isolation, and Elena (D) found hide preparation repulsive.

questions.question: acknowledges the specific external factor or media that led to their involvement?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) saw a documentary, and Elena (D) a community center advertisement.
